title: cmd: remove SkipArgReorder
body:
Fixes #940 

Before:
```
➜  buildah git:(from-issue) ✗ sudo buildah from scratch --name builder 
too many arguments specified
```
After:
```
➜  buildah git:(from-issue) ✗ sudo ./buildah from scratch --name builder 
builder
➜  buildah git:(from-issue) ✗ sudo buildah containers    
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
e173e4ed21d3     *                  scratch                          builder

```

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body:
@q384566678, thanks a lot for the PR. Although it fixes the issue, I have some doubts if that's the way to go.

As mentioned in #940, the image is required to be the last argument to `buildah-from`, which is not the case with the changes in this PR anymore. I am also skeptical towards the reordering of arguments as it can introduce very subtle bugs and some CLI invocations can turn into something quite ambiguous.

Thanks for the PR @q384566678 , but sorry, I concur with @vrothberg .  We don't want to remove the reorder flag for a number of reasons that he stated.  For the `buildah from` command the last argument should be the image name.  We need to "just" change the error returned in this instance, but I quote the just as I'm not sure it will be a quick and easy fix.
I agree.  We want to be consistent and have the reorder flag in all the tools
